verses.commentary: Population growth sets the stage for the corruption narrative that follows.

The sons of God (בְנֵי־הָאֱלֹהִים) remain one of Scripture's mysteries. Interpretations vary: fallen angels, godly line of Seth, or powerful rulers. Regardless, boundary violation occurs—taking without restraint, crossing lines God established.
God limits human lifespan to 120 years, whether as countdown to the flood or new maximum age. Divine patience has limits. We are flesh, not spirit.
The Nephilim (נְפִלִים, fallen ones) were products of this union. The text demythologizes ancient legends—they're not gods, just powerful mortals in a corrupted world.
Total depravity: every inclination continually evil. Not occasional sin but systematic wickedness. This justifies what follows. God sees the heart, not just actions.
God's grief is stunning. The Creator regrets (nacham, נָחַם) creating humanity—not divine mistake but divine sorrow. God's heart can break over human evil.
The decision to destroy includes animals because they're part of human stewardship. When humanity fails, creation suffers. Foreshadows Romans 8:20-22.
But. The most important word. Noah found favor (חֵן, grace). Not earned but given. One righteous person changes everything. Grace interrupts judgment.
Noah walked with God like Enoch (5:22). Blameless among contemporaries doesn't mean sinless but faithful and obedient in a corrupt generation.
His three sons will repopulate the earth. The number three recurs in Scripture's new beginnings.
Corruption (שָׁחַת) and violence (חָמָס) fill the earth. Not just sin but systemic breakdown. Creation returns toward chaos.
Universal indictment: all flesh corrupted its way. God's assessment is total.
The same Hebrew word for corrupt (שָׁחַת) now means destroy. Measure for measure—their corruption brings their destruction.
The ark (תֵּבָה) is not a ship but a box, a floating chest. No rudder, no sail—God will steer it. The word for pitch (כֹּפֶר) relates to atonement (כַּפָּרָה), a covering that saves.
Roughly 450 x 75 x 45 feet with 6:1 length-to-width ratio—ideal for stability. Modern naval engineering confirms this design's seaworthiness.
One door, one way in. This prefigures salvation through Christ (John 10:9). God provides the entrance.
The flood (mabbul, מַבּוּל) is unique, used only of Noah's flood. Total judgment on all breathing flesh. Only those in the ark survive.
First mention of covenant (berit, בְּרִית) in Scripture. God commits Himself to Noah—covenant is God's initiative. Eight people will be saved.
Noah becomes a new Adam, stewarding creation's preservation. God's plan includes animals, not just humans.
God brings the animals to Noah. Divine provision, not human effort. The creatures come willingly.
Provision for survival. Faith requires practical action and preparation.
Perfect obedience. No recorded questions or objections. Noah does exactly what God says—this is the heart of righteousness.
